vulnerability.reason: Generated condition describes reliance on aggregate metrics obscuring bias detection, while reference describes SGD training amplifying majority patterns and overshadowing minority signals; related but different sensitivity.
vulnerability.reference: Standard SGD training procedures can amplify majority subpopulation patterns, overshadowing minority subgroups' signals.
vulnerability.generated: Researchers' reliance on held-out aggregate performance metrics obscures subpopulation-specific biases, leaving them unable to detect skewed model behavior.

impact.reason: Both mention minority subgroups as affected party, but generated damage type is misrepresentation and contaminated analyses, not inequitable resource allocation or health disparities.
impact.reference: Erroneous subgroup predictions result in inequitable resource allocation and exacerbate health disparities.
impact.generated: Scientific knowledge becomes distorted, with minority subpopulations misrepresented and downstream analyses contaminated by uncontrolled biases.

dose_response.reason: Generated causal arc relies on aggregate metrics obscuring biases, not SGD favoring majority patterns; the mechanism and outcome (distorted knowledge vs. erroneous predictions and health disparities) are entirely different.
dose_response.reference: Expressive ML models' amplification of statistical biases enables SGD to favor majority patterns, skewing model insights and leading to erroneous subgroup predictions and health disparities.
dose_response.generated: Expressive ML models amplifying biases through unvalidated flexibility and selective held-out evaluation, when researchers analyze subpopulation behavior, trigger reliance on aggregate metrics that obscures subpopulation biases, distorting scientific knowledge and misrepresenting minority subpopulations.
